title: Committed review catches conditional required-field docs parity gaps

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark that committed review preserves conditional required-field validator-to-doc parity findings whenever the resource docs page is in scope.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models a provider review where docs and validator logic are both in scope. The benchmark is that review should catch a missing conditional requirement note rather than treating the docs as complete because the field is mentioned somewhere on the page.

description: A synthetic committed-review scenario where validator logic requires a field for specific mode values, but the companion docs describe the field without documenting the conditional requirement. The correct review must surface the validator-to-doc parity gap when the docs page is in scope.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models a provider review where docs and validator logic are both in scope. The benchmark is that review should catch a missing conditional requirement note rather than treating the docs as complete because the field is mentioned somewhere on the page.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should flag that the validator requires `duration_value` when `duration_mode` is `AlwaysOverride` or `OverrideWhenMissing`, but the resource docs do not currently document that conditional requirement.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not treat the docs as complete merely because `duration_value` is described somewhere on the page when the conditional blocking requirement is still omitted.

notes: Use this case to benchmark that committed review preserves conditional required-field validator-to-doc parity findings whenever the resource docs page is in scope.
